title: trichocline heterophylla var. humilis
intro: Trichocline heterophylla var. humilis, also known as a variety of the Trichocline heterophylla plant, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is native to New Zealand and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations that aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps. Herbal preparations of Trichocline heterophylla var. humilis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
